title: Core switch fabric degradation causing packet loss on VLAN 200
description: Nexus 9000 core switch pair experiencing 3-5% packet loss on VLAN 200 (production application tier). Affecting 45 application servers. Root cause traced to outdated NX-OS firmware with known bug CSCvz12345. Temporary mitigation via traffic redistribution across backup path.
resolution: Traffic rerouted via backup path. Permanent fix requires NX-OS upgrade from 9.3(8) to 10.2(5) on both core switches during maintenance window.

title: BGP session flapping with ISP causing intermittent internet outages
description: Primary BGP peering session with AT&T dropping every 15-20 minutes. 200+ users experiencing 30-60 second internet outages during failover to secondary ISP. Issue started after ISP maintenance. MTU mismatch suspected on ISP side.
resolution: Temporary fix: reduced MTU to 1400 on edge router interface. ISP confirmed MTU issue on their PE router, fix scheduled for their next maintenance window.

title: Wireless controller failover failure leaving Building B without WiFi
description: Primary WLC (WLC-01) crashed due to memory exhaustion from rogue AP detection module. Secondary WLC (WLC-02) failed to assume control of 120 access points in Building B due to stale HA configuration. 350 users without WiFi for 2 hours.
resolution: Manual failover performed. HA configuration resynchronized. Memory leak in rogue AP module tracked to firmware bug — upgrade needed.

title: Firewall rule misconfiguration blocking SAP traffic after change window
description: After scheduled firewall policy update on FW-PROD-01, implicit deny rule blocked SAP RFC traffic on ports 3300-3399. Finance team unable to process month-end close for 4 hours. Change review process failed to catch SAP dependency.
resolution: Emergency change to add SAP RFC allow rule. Root cause: change request did not include SAP traffic flow documentation. CAB review process needs improvement.

title: DNS resolution failures during datacenter UPS maintenance
description: Both internal DNS servers (DNS-01, DNS-02) in DC-1 lost power simultaneously during UPS battery replacement. 500+ clients unable to resolve internal hostnames for 8 minutes. External DNS failover to cloud DNS worked but internal zones were unavailable.
resolution: DNS restored after UPS maintenance. Need to deploy DNS-03 in DC-2 for geographic redundancy. Internal DNS should not have single-site dependency.

title: VPN concentrator reaching 95% session capacity during remote work surge
description: GlobalProtect VPN concentrator VPN-01 hit 950 of 1000 session limit during snowstorm work-from-home event. New connections being refused. VPN-02 (backup) not configured for load sharing, only cold standby.
resolution: Temporary: increased session limit to 1200 (within hardware capacity). Need active-active VPN configuration and capacity planning for 1500+ concurrent sessions.

title: MPLS circuit between HQ and DR site showing elevated latency
description: MPLS circuit MPLS-HQ-DR showing 45ms latency (normally 12ms) and 0.1% packet loss. Database replication between sites falling behind. ISP reports fiber degradation on intermediate node. Affecting DR readiness SLA.
resolution: ISP dispatched fiber repair crew. Temporary traffic shifted to backup internet VPN tunnel. DR replication caught up within 2 hours of reroute.

title: Network monitoring blind spot — 30 switches missing from SNMP polling
description: Quarterly audit revealed 30 access-layer switches not being monitored by SolarWinds. Switches were deployed 6 months ago but never added to monitoring. Two of these switches had failing power supplies that went undetected.
resolution: All 30 switches added to monitoring. Two PSU replacements ordered. Need automated discovery and onboarding process for new network devices.
